name: Terry, Sarah
resolved_employer: Burnett Specialists (Houston) — Operations Manager for IT/Engineering at the Texas staffing/recruiting firm; the filing also lists SmartWorx Global LLC, a staffing/recruiting and IT-consulting firm operating in Austin, Houston and San Antonio (apparent later employer)
evidence: A Sarah Terry is documented at Burnett Specialists' Houston office as Operations Manager for IT/Engineering — she placed 6th in her category at the 40th Texas Staffing & Recruiting Services Association awards, and a Yelp reviewer describes interviewing with 'Sarah Terry' for an engineering position there. That matches the filing's 'Recruiter' occupation, the Burnett Specialists employer string, and the 77008 (Houston Heights) zip; the second employer string, SmartWorx Global LLC, is likewise a Houston/Austin staffing and recruiting firm, so both employers are consistent with one recruiting career. Held at medium because 'Sarah Terry' is a common name and no source directly ties her to the SmartWorx role. Classified Consulting / PR as the nearest taxonomy label for staffing/professional services. Note the pattern: a Houston resident giving to Austin council member Vanessa Fuentes 2020-2023, against a heavy and uniformly Democratic federal record (135 FEC gifts, lean 1.0).

name: Groepler, Paul
resolved_employer: G2 Advisors, Inc. — President and Director (Austin); also an independent consultant with Applied Economics Consulting Group, Inc., an Austin boutique litigation-consulting and economic expert-services firm, and a partner consultant to Guggenheim Venture Partners
evidence: Paul F. Groepler is recorded as president and director of G2 Advisors, Inc. (registered agent address 4300 Avenue D, Austin 78751), matching the filing's employer string exactly, and public-records listings place his residence at 4202 Bradwood Rd, Austin TX 78722 — an exact match to the donor's 78722 zip. He works as an independent consultant with Applied Economics Consulting Group (economic/financial advisory for business litigation, valuation and public policy) and has served as a partner consultant to Guggenheim Venture Partners; his earlier career spans Dell, Motorola, Sun Microsystems, Daimler-Benz and IBM Research. Occupation 'Consultant' matches. The exact zip plus the distinctive employer name make this a confident identification.

name: Freeland, Peter
resolved_employer: Unbundled Capital — Founder and Managing Partner; an Austin-based permanent-capital investment firm making growth investments in vertical B2B SaaS, embedded payments and marketplaces (Occupier, Findd.AI). Previously an operator/investor at Skimmer, DoctorLogic and Fiix Software, and earlier at TA Associates and General Catalyst Partners
evidence: Peter Freeland is the founder and managing partner of Unbundled Capital, an Austin permanent-capital investment firm, and is publicly described as an Austin-based entrepreneur and investor specializing in high-growth B2B SaaS. The filing's employer string 'Unbundled Capital' and occupations 'technology investor and entrepreneur' / 'Investor' match exactly, and the 78703 (Tarrytown/central Austin) zip fits his stated Austin base. His firm led a $16M growth investment in Occupier (June 2025) and a $21M investment in Findd.AI.

name: Chapman, Kevin
resolved_employer: Boon-Chapman (Austin) — Chairman of the Board; a family-owned third-party administrator of employee benefits and self-funded health plans, founded 1961 by his father Thurman Chapman and W. Robert Boon and the oldest domiciled TPA in Texas (700+ clients, 75,000+ covered employees). Also co-founded sister company Soluta in 2010
evidence: Kevin Chapman, J.D., GBA, is Chairman of the Board of Boon-Chapman and is based in Austin, matching the filing's employer strings ('BoonChapman', 'Boon-Chapman'), the 'Business Owner' occupation and the 78731 (northwest/central Austin) zip. He is the son of co-founder Thurman Chapman and took over the firm on his father's retirement; the company is licensed by the Texas Department of Insurance as a TPA and BBB-listed as an insurance agency, so it is classified Finance (insurance) rather than Healthcare. Worth noting for pattern: his federal giving is uniformly Republican (38 FEC gifts, lean 0.0) while these 2022-2024 gifts went to Democrat Kirk Watson.

name: Saenz, Gina
evidence: Could not identify. The filing self-reports 'US Ambassador' / 'United States HSTAN Ambassador' at 'Healing to the Nations' and 'Healing Streams Ambassadors Network'. Both strings point to evangelical ministry volunteer titles rather than employment: 'Healing Streams' is the global healing-service broadcast of Pastor Chris Oyakhilome's Christ Embassy / Believers' LoveWorld ministry, and Healing To The Nations Ministries is a separate evangelistic nonprofit founded in 2001 by Winthrop and Lilly Jeyasingham. Neither organization publishes an ambassador roster, and no Gina Saenz in Austin 78748 could be tied to either — the only Texas Gina Saenz profiles found are in Brownsville and Houston. Since 'ambassador' here is an unverifiable self-reported ministry title and not a confirmed occupation, no industry is assigned. Gifts are recent (2025-09 to 2026-03) and all to George Morales (Travis County Pct 4).

name: Doughty, Ron
resolved_employer: Prudential Financial / Prudential Insurance — Vice President, External Affairs (Austin); a registered Texas lobbyist, listed by the Texas Ethics Commission as 'Doughty, Ronald H. (The Honorable)', business classification Corporate Executive, at 8601 Ranch Road 2222, Bldg 1, Austin TX 78730 (TEC FilerID 25893), appearing on the registered-lobbyist lists from at least 2007 through 2020
evidence: A Ron Doughty is listed as Vice President of External Affairs at Prudential Insurance based in Austin, TX, and the Texas Ethics Commission's registered-lobbyist lists carry a Ronald H. Doughty in Austin classified as a Corporate Executive — the two are consistent with one person doing in-house government affairs for Prudential in the state capital. The donor's 78732 (Steiner Ranch) zip is a residential area a few miles from the 78730 Ranch Road 2222 business address on the lobby registration. Held at medium rather than high because the filing lists no occupation or employer of its own, so the identification rests on name plus Austin geography rather than on a self-reported employer match, and the TEC entry does not itself name Prudential in the sources reviewed. Gives to both Kirk Watson (city) and Brigid Shea (county), 2022-2025, with a very heavy and uniformly Democratic federal record (168 FEC gifts, lean 1.0). Note the TEC 'The Honorable' prefix implies prior elected or appointed office, which could not be pinned down.

affiliations.role: VP, External Affairs — registered Texas lobbyist (Texas Ethics Commission, business classification 'Corporate Executive'), i.e. in-house government-affairs representative for a major insurance and asset-management company

name: Tucci, Marc
resolved_employer: Austin Sports & Social Club — Founder and CEO; adult recreational co-ed sports-league operator founded 2005, which with its affiliates (including Lone Star Sports & Social Club) serves 120,000+ members across Austin, San Antonio, Dallas/Fort Worth and Minneapolis/St. Paul
evidence: Marc Tucci is the founder and CEO of the Austin Sports & Social Club — he moved to Texas specifically to start it in 2005 — and is listed as CEO on the Sport & Social Industry Association's contacts roster and interviewed as founder/CEO on Austin's Soccer Pod. The filing's employer strings ('Austin Sports & Social Club', 'Lone Star Sports & Social Club', 'CEO') and occupations ('CEO', 'Owner', 'Austin Sports and Social Club') match exactly, and the 78739 (Circle C, southwest Austin) zip is consistent. Classified Hospitality / Events as an operator of social recreational leagues and events.

name: Flathouse, Virgil
evidence: Partially identified as a civic figure, but no occupation could be established. Dr. Virgil Flathouse is a former Eanes ISD trustee and served as Chair of the district's 2015 Bond Oversight Committee. Eanes ISD is the school district for West Lake Hills, matching the donor's West Lake Hills 78746 address, and 'Flathouse' is a rare surname with essentially no other Central Texas presence, so the identification of the person is likely sound. However, the filing lists no occupation or employer, and no source establishes what his 'Dr.' credential or career is (searches against physician, dentist, professor and superintendent listings returned nothing), so no industry is assigned. School-board service is an unpaid elected role and is recorded below as a civic affiliation rather than as employment. Gives to Ann Howard (Travis County Pct 3), 2022-2025.

affiliations.role: Former member, Board of Trustees; Chair of the 2015 Bond Oversight Committee

name: Lash, John
resolved_employer: Farm to Table (Farm to Table Texas) — Founder and Owner; an Austin farm-to-restaurant produce distribution and delivery company he started in 2008 in his wife's old Chevy Suburban, now serving 500+ clients across Austin, San Antonio and the Hill Country (La Condesa, Lenoir, Tacodeli, P. Terry's, Kerbey Lane) and expanding into Houston
evidence: John Lash founded Farm to Table in Austin in 2008 and is its owner, running weekly routes of several hundred miles through Central Texas to bring produce, meat and cheese from family farmers to restaurant chefs; he is profiled by name as the company's founder in CultureMap Austin and listed on the firm's own team page. The filing's employer string 'Farm to Table' and 'Owner' occupation match exactly, and the 78756 (Brentwood/central Austin) zip is consistent. Classified Retail as the nearest taxonomy label for food wholesale/distribution. Gives to Mike Siegel (city) and Brigid Shea (county), 2024-2026.

name: Graham, Charles
evidence: Could not identify — the record carries no zip, no location, no occupation and no employer, and 'Charles Graham' is an extremely common name, so there is nothing to corroborate against. The only distinguishing signal is the federal donation history: 651 FEC gifts with a 0.24 partisan lean, i.e. a very high-volume, predominantly Republican donor who made a single $550 gift to Democrat Jeff Travillion (Travis County Pct 1) on 2021-11-08. That pattern is notable but is not by itself identifying, and the 'Charles Graham' who appears in FEC records as a candidate is a North Carolina state legislator, unrelated. No industry assigned.

name: Ma, Muyuan
resolved_employer: Wilsonart International Holdings, LLC / Wilsonart LLC — Senior Research Fellow; Wilsonart is globally headquartered in Austin and manufactures engineered surfaces (high-pressure laminate, quartz, solid surface, TFL and edgebanding)
evidence: Muyuan Ma is a Senior Research Fellow at Wilsonart International Holdings in the Austin metropolitan area, and Muyuan M. Ma is a named inventor on Justia-indexed patents for decorative laminate lay-ups, UV-curable textured coating compositions on decorative paper layers, and textured press plates used in laminate manufacture — squarely the work of a laminate research chemist. This matches the filing's employer strings ('Wilsonart LLC', 'Wilsonart LLC / home') and occupations ('Scientist', 'Chemist') exactly, and Wilsonart's Austin global headquarters corroborates the 78717 (northwest Austin/Cedar Park) zip. Note this is a distinct person from the same-named Northeastern University chemical-engineering undergraduate. Classified Engineering as the closest taxonomy label for materials-science R&D; the taxonomy has no manufacturing category.

name: Friedrich, Miguel
resolved_employer: nTact — Chief Business Development Officer (previously VP of Sales and Marketing); nTact is a maker of precision slot-die coating equipment for flat-panel display, semiconductor, solar and solid-state lighting applications
evidence: Miguel Friedrich is listed on nTact's own management page and across professional directories as the company's Chief Business Development Officer, matching the filing's employer string 'nTact' and occupations 'Chief Business Development Officer' / 'CBDO' exactly. He is a multilingual international business executive with roughly twenty years' experience opening markets in flat-panel display, semiconductor, solar and solid-state lighting, holds an MBA from SMU's Cox School of Business (2011-2013), and previously worked in investment banking on M&A and corporate restructuring. The 75013 Allen, TX zip fits nTact's Dallas-area base. Notable pattern: a Dallas-area donor giving to Austin council member Natasha Harper-Madison in the 2018 cycle only. His solar-coating work (e.g. DOE perovskite programs) is clean-energy equipment rather than fossil energy, so no oil & gas flag applies.
